diagrams.title: JSP Implicit Objects Architecture
diagrams.description: A block diagram showing the eight implicit objects available in JSP pages and their relationship to the servlet container
elements: Eight rectangular blocks representing: request (HttpServletRequest), response (HttpServletResponse), out (JspWriter), session (HttpSession), application (ServletContext), config (ServletConfig), pageContext (PageContext), page (Object - this)
Arrow from 'client' to 'request' showing HTTP request flow
Arrow from 'request' to 'pageContext' showing page context scope
Arrow from 'pageContext' to 'session' showing session access
Arrow from 'pageContext' to 'application' showing application scope
Container box labeled 'Servlet Container (Tomcat)' encompassing all objects
diagrams.purpose: Students learn the eight built-in objects available in JSP without explicit declaration, understanding how they relate to servlet API and the servlet container environment

diagrams.title: JSP Variable Scope Hierarchy
diagrams.description: A layered diagram showing the four variable scopes in JSP from narrowest to widest
elements: Innermost circle labeled 'pageScope' (only in single JSP page)
Second circle labeled 'requestScope' (available during request processing)
Third circle labeled 'sessionScope' (available for user session)
Outermost circle labeled 'applicationScope' (shared across all users)
Arrows showing data flow: page → request → session → application
Labels on each level showing corresponding implicit objects: pageContext, request, session, application
diagrams.purpose: Students understand how variables declared in JSP persist across different contexts and how scope determines accessibility and lifetime of variables

diagrams.title: Servlet to JSP Data Flow
diagrams.description: Flow diagram showing how data moves from servlet to JSP using request attributes, session attributes, and application attributes
elements: Rectangle labeled 'Servlet' with sub-blocks: 'doGet/doPost', 'setAttribute()'
Arrow from Servlet to 'HttpServletRequest' labeled 'request.setAttribute()'
Arrow from Servlet to 'HttpSession' labeled 'session.setAttribute()'
Arrow from Servlet to 'ServletContext' labeled 'application.setAttribute()'
Arrow from each to rectangle labeled 'JSP Page' with sub-blocks: 'request.getAttribute()', 'session.getAttribute()', 'application.getAttribute()'
Dotted box labeled 'Forward/Include from Servlet'
diagrams.purpose: Students learn how servlets and JSP pages share data using attribute methods, essential for Model-View-Controller architecture

diagrams.title: JSP Scripting Elements and Variable Types
diagrams.description: Classification diagram showing different ways to declare and use variables in JSP pages
elements: Main box divided into four sections: 'Declaration (<%! %>)', 'Scriptlet (<% %>)', 'Expression (<%= %>)', 'EL (${})'
Under Declaration: 'Instance variables', 'Methods', 'Lifecycle methods (jspInit/jspDestroy)'
Under Scriptlet: 'Local variables', 'Control statements', 'Java code blocks'
Under Expression: 'Output values', 'Automatic toString()', 'Expressions evaluation'
Under EL: 'Accessing attributes', 'Arithmetic/logical operations', 'Implicit objects'
diagrams.purpose: Students understand the different scripting elements in JSP and how each affects variable declaration and usage

diagrams.title: Cookie and Session Variable Flow
diagrams.description: Diagram showing how cookies and session variables track user data across multiple requests
elements: Client browser rectangle
Arrow from browser labeled 'HTTP Request + Cookie: JSESSIONID' to 'Servlet Container'
Servlet Container box containing 'Session Object (Map<String, Object>)'
Arrow from session to browser labeled 'Set-Cookie: JSESSIONID'
Rectangle showing 'session.setAttribute("user", userObj)' and 'session.getAttribute("user")'
Time label showing session timeout concept
diagrams.purpose: Students learn how session variables maintain user state across requests using cookies and server-side session storage

charts.title: JSP Implicit Objects Comparison Table
charts.content: Table comparing all 8 implicit objects: object name, interface type, scope, common methods, and usage examples. Columns: Implicit Object | Interface | Scope | Key Methods | Example Use. Rows for: request, response, out, session, application, config, pageContext, page

charts.title: Variable Declaration Methods in JSP
charts.content: Comparison of four methods: Scriptlet Declaration (<% int x=5; %>), JSP Declaration (<%! int x=5; %>), JSP Expression (<%= x %>), Expression Language (${x}). Columns: Method | Syntax | Scope | When to Use | Example

charts.title: Request Parameter Processing Flow
charts.content: Flowchart showing: Client Form → HTTP Request → request.getParameter() → Variable Assignment → JSP Display. Decision diamonds for: null check, data validation, data type conversion

charts.title: Attribute vs Parameter in JSP/Servlets
charts.content: Two-column comparison: 'Request Parameters' (from HTML form, URL query, getParameter(), always String) vs 'Request Attributes' (set by servlet via setAttribute(), can be any Object, passed between components)

branches: Implicit Objects (8 built-in objects: request, response, session, application, out, pageContext, config, page)
Explicit Variables (scriptlet declarations, expression language, JSP declarations)
Servlet Integration (request dispatching, attribute sharing, forward/include)
mindMaps.purpose: Provides comprehensive overview connecting JSP variable concepts with servlet API, showing how implicit objects, explicit declarations, and servlet integration work together for dynamic web application development

branches: Session Tracking (session API, cookies, URL rewriting)
mindMaps.purpose: Shows how data flows between JSP pages and servlets, essential for understanding Model-View-Controller architecture in web applications
